signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + PPI Surprise
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5852. The G10 Currency Strength Scorecard triggers a critical alert as NZD outperforms peers by +1.322% (n=4 pairs) while the USD lags at -0.715% (n=7 pairs), generating a 2.037% leader-to-laggard spread. Fed 3.75%. Despite a -125bp carry deficit (RBNZ 2.5% vs Fed 3.75%), NZD/USD is supported by RBNZ's active hiking bias and a crowded NZD speculative short position (COT LF net short -27,675), creating short-squeeze risk. Watch for the Fed speaker Musalem on 16 July to confirm or challenge this dollar-weakness regime.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.124. Despite a massive +275bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%), USD/JPY faces structural headwinds as US military strikes on Iran escalate geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. The options market is actively hedging downside risk, with the 1M 25-Delta Risk Reversal showing a moderate put skew of -1.39 (Saxo, 1M tenor, indicative mid) and USD/JPY implied volatility at 7.2% vs 3.5% realized (HV30), yielding an elevated +3.7% Vol Risk Premium. This options protection demand contradicts the crowded speculative carry trade (JPY COT LF net short -104,231). A sustained break below 162.00 targets 160.00, while the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 July remains the primary policy catalyst.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — Retail Sentiment vs COT Divergence
signals.text: GBP/JPY at 219.526. Retail sentiment on Myfxbook shows an extreme 87% short positioning, which directly diverges from institutional positioning where GBP COT LF net long stands at +17,228 and JPY COT LF net short is highly crowded at -104,231. This extreme retail-to-institutional divergence, combined with a +275bp carry premium (BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%), signals a high probability of a contrarian retail capitulation squeeze. The options market aligns with this upside bias, pricing a moderate put skew on EUR/JPY (-1.42) but keeping GBP/JPY supported via SONIA-TONA carry. Watch for GBP/JPY to hold above 219.00 to confirm continuation of the institutional long trend.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Vol Complacency
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4035. The 30-day rolling correlation between USD/CAD and WTI Oil has broken down to -0.906 vs its 252-day historical norm of +0.042 (z = -2.04σ). This extreme negative correlation indicates that the traditional CAD-oil link is highly active as WTI rises +1.21% to $80.3 on Middle East supply risks, dragging USD/CAD lower despite a +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%). Speculative positioning remains heavily short CAD (COT LF net short -87,846), leaving the pair vulnerable to a rapid long-unwind if oil prices extend gains. Watch for a break below 1.4000 to confirm the correlation-driven trend reversal.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.35410. The options market is pricing an elevated volatility risk premium, with GBP/USD ATM IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.5%, representing a +4.5% VRP. This high hedging demand occurs despite a neutral nominal carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75% -> +0bp) and a moderate negative Economic Surprise Index (GBP CESI net -7 misses). Speculators remain net long GBP (COT LF net long +17,228), but the high VRP and mild put skew (-0.62) suggest institutional traders are paying up for downside protection against a potential long-unwind. Watch the next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 Jul to resolve this volatility premium.
